slides.title: Module Lattice + NIST and Brainpool Curves Composites in OpenPGP (draft-ietf-openpgp-nist-bp-comp)

chat.author: Corinne Cath
chat.text: dkg has a comment or just generally looks focused and critical :p

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: DKIM keys are almost never changed though

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: there's also DKIM2 in development; HKP probably doesn't need anthing from that so referrring to the current DKIM RFC is  probably fine for us

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: ok, so this is motivated by minimizing accidental backscatter, not by preventing or minimizing deliberate abuse

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: GnuPG uses an experimental codepoint somewhere else down deeper in the wire format framing

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: i'm not saying that i think it's useful to change the subkey usage flags for a given key over time

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: i actually don't have a use case where that would be a good idea

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: and i agree that the cost isn't worth the absent benefit

chat.author: Daniel Huigens
chat.text: Or preserving the original signature creation timestamp when updating it (even though that's a bit of a hack)

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: Daniel, taking that route makes it harder to tell which signature is more recent

chat.author: Daniel Huigens
chat.text: Yeah, that's true. Sequoia has a t+1 second "hack" for that reason
